title: Planning and control tables made database-native
owner: LifeOS Planning Spine / LifeOS Data Agent
objective: Define the PostgreSQL-native control plane for the Planning Spine so that every planning question is answered by a SQL query and every accepted answer is an UPDATE to a database row, while the committed CSV, JSON, and Markdown surfaces become deterministic, byte-identical, lossless projections of those rows. This plan assigns a database owner and a deterministic projection contract to every current control-plane column, relation, proof URI, lifecycle transition, North Star anchor, and source provenance with zero information loss.

supporting_sources: 1.0_VISION/Notebooklm/Architecture Context Matrix_ PostgreSQL-First LifeOS Framework - Table 1.csv
grounding.parent_task: POSTGRES-002 (whole user-space relational graph and identity model). This plan reuses the same lossless-identity discipline for the control plane itself rather than user-space content.

store_alignment.control_plane_state_class: durable relational macro-state (identities, histories, permissions, provenance, causal dependency edges)
store_alignment.why: The Planning Spine control plane is durable relational macro-state (task identities, lifecycle histories, provenance lineage, anchor->task causal edges, proof audit trail). STORE-001 assigns this exact class canonical ownership to PostgreSQL + RuVector; the Architecture Context Matrix (row 1) classifies PostgreSQL as 'Global knowledge base, relational data, and shared knowledge graphs' and 'canonical state', and the Architecture Blueprint tiered-memory table lists PostgreSQL persistence as 'Enterprise ACID / PITR Recovery'.
store_alignment.projection_class: derived projections (materialized files regenerated deterministically from PostgreSQL rows via envctl). STORE-001 assigns this class canonical ownership to PostgreSQL projected by envctl (read-only, forward-only, disposable). The committed .csv/.json/.md control surfaces are these projections.
store_alignment.redb_role: transient only. Per STORE-001 and the Blueprint, redb is the microsecond forward buffer / WAL used during the one-time ingest (redb -> envctl embed -> PostgreSQL); it is never a second durable writer of any control-plane relation. During normal operation the control plane is written directly to PostgreSQL.
store_alignment.codedb_role: nu_plugin CodeDB is the parser/ingestion path only. It parses the current committed CSV/JSON/MD control surfaces into structured blocks buffered in redb; envctl bridges them into PostgreSQL. CodeDB owns no durable control-plane authority.
store_alignment.ruvector_role: RuVector (native No-Sidecar PostgreSQL extension) hosts semantic embeddings of goal / verification_gate / proof_summary text so planning questions can be answered by hybrid SQL + vector search ('ORDER BY semantic_embedding <-> ...'), per Blueprint section 5.
store_alignment.cutover_guard: This is a PLANNING artifact. The currently committed generated/task_graph.source.csv and proof_records/proof_ledger.jsonl remain the canonical editable authority until POSTGRES-010 proves whole-user-space parity and performs one explicit verified authority switch. No live PostgreSQL was created or mutated; product_execution_performed is false.

control_plane_inventory.note: The six gate categories, each enumerated from the real current control-plane surfaces. Every item below is assigned a database owner and a deterministic projection contract in the following sections.

1_columns.description: Every column of every current control-plane relation, with its owning PostgreSQL table.column, lossless representation rule, and projection rule. Multi-value ('; '-delimited) columns are normalized into ordinal child tables and re-serialized byte-identically on projection.

task_graph_source_csv.csv_dialect: RFC4180, QUOTE_ALL (every field quoted), UTF-8, LF line endings, header row verbatim

columns.lossless_rule: normalized to parent_ids array in normalized.json (multi-parent exists, e.g. LIFEOS-010 -> LIFEOS-007;LIFEOS-008;LIFEOS-009); ordinal preserves order; empty parent stored as zero edges
columns.projection: GROUP_CONCAT parent_task_id ORDER BY ordinal SEPARATOR '; '

columns.lossless_rule: 27 distinct phases enumerated in phase table verbatim
verbatim including ' / ' separators (kept as free text, not split, to guarantee byte-identity)
verbatim; RuVector embedding stored in task_node.goal_embedding for semantic query
split on '; ' into ordered rows; re-joined with '; '
columns.projection: GROUP_CONCAT input_ref ORDER BY ordinal SEPARATOR '; '

columns.lossless_rule: split on '; ' into ordered rows; re-joined with '; '
columns.projection: GROUP_CONCAT artifact_path ORDER BY ordinal SEPARATOR '; '

columns.lossless_rule: split on '; ' into ordered rows; re-joined with '; '
columns.projection: GROUP_CONCAT path_glob ORDER BY ordinal SEPARATOR '; '

columns.lossless_rule: split on '; ' into ordered rows; re-joined with '; '
columns.projection: GROUP_CONCAT blocked_clause ORDER BY ordinal SEPARATOR '; '

columns.lossless_rule: TRUE/FALSE stored as boolean; projected as uppercase literal 'TRUE'/'FALSE' to match current CSV
columns.projection: CASE simulation_required WHEN true THEN 'TRUE' ELSE 'FALSE' END

columns.lossless_rule: MUTABLE authored field; one of exactly three title-case values observed in the sheet

columns.type: text (soft FK -> proof_record.proof_uri or source_extract path)

task_graph_source_csv.mutable_column_authority: Mirrors task_graph.status.json update_policy.allowed_updated_fields exactly. All other 14 columns are authored-immutable in the sheet (update_policy.mutates_source_graph = false).

north_star_coverage_source_csv.stable_ordering_key: ns_anchor.anchor_ordinal (canonical order NS-EXTENSION, NS-WHOLE, NS-CLARITY, NS-DIRECTION, NS-PROGRESS)

columns.lossless_rule: split on '; ' into ordered rows; re-joined with '; '
columns.projection: GROUP_CONCAT task_id ORDER BY ordinal SEPARATOR '; '

columns.lossless_rule: verbatim (e.g. closed-until-full-graph); drives coverage-control global gate

columns.owner: proof_live_command(task_id, revision, ordinal, purpose, cwd, command, exit_status, output jsonb)
task_family_work_item(task_id, item_id, ordinal, surface, owner, decision, capture jsonb, restore text)

source_registry_csv.owner: source table (PK object_id) + source_task_map(object_id, ordinal, task_id) for the '; '-delimited mapped_task_ids column

adopted_capabilities_csv.owner: adopted_capability table (PK capability); store_role FK -> store_class_assignment

store_decision_json.owner: store_decision (PK task_id='STORE-001') + store_class_assignment(class, canonical_owner, write_authority, sync_direction, recovery_source, migration_path) + store_invariant(ordinal, text)

2_relations.description: Every current control-plane relation gets an owning PostgreSQL table. 31 tables total (core control plane + ordinal-normalization + provenance).

3_proof_uris.description: Every proof URI referenced by the control plane has a database owner.

kinds.owner: proof_record (keyed by task_id, revision); the task_node.proof_uri column is a soft FK
kinds.count_reference: 196 task rows each carry a proof_uri; 118 point at accepted proof records

kinds.owner: source_claim / source (the NBSTATED-* family points proof_uri at the extract that proves the verbatim capture)
kinds.note: these are provenance URIs, not proof records; owned by the source relation

kinds.owner: proof_ledger_entry.proof_uri (soft FK -> proof_record) with proof_ledger_entry.proof_sha256 pinning content identity

3_proof_uris.integrity_rule: Every proof_uri string that resolves to proof_records/*.proof.json MUST have a matching proof_record row whose recomputed SHA-256 equals the proof_ledger_entry.proof_sha256 for that URI. Dangling proof_uris are a projection-validation failure.

4_lifecycle_transitions.description: The Planning Spine lifecycle state machine. Seven canonical states (from task_graph.status.json.lifecycle_counts) plus the transition edges are owned in the database; lifecycle_state per task is a DETERMINISTIC DERIVATION (not an authored column), computed from task_node.status + proof presence + rollback events + coverage gate.

states.meaning: authored, not yet eligible or executing
dependencies complete and coverage gate open; eligible to run
simulation_required task whose simulation passed, awaiting proof
accepted proof recorded in the ledger
a dependency failed or the coverage gate is closed

csv_status_to_lifecycle_map.note: The task_graph.source.csv status column holds only {Draft, Running, Complete}. The richer 7-state lifecycle is derived. This mapping is owned by the projection layer so both the coarse authored status and the fine derived state round-trip.
csv_status_to_lifecycle_map.Draft: draft | ready | blocked (refined by dependency completion + ns_anchor.execution_gate)
csv_status_to_lifecycle_map.Running: running | simulated (refined by task_node.simulation_required and simulation result)
csv_status_to_lifecycle_map.Complete: complete | rolled-back (refined by presence of a rollback proof_ledger_entry)

transitions.guard: all task_dependency parents in state complete AND governing ns_anchor.execution_gate resolves open
a parent is blocked/rolled-back OR global_coverage_gate = closed
executor claims the task; task_node.status set to 'Running'
accepted proof_record inserted; proof_ledger_entry appended; task_node.status set to 'Complete', proof_uri + next_action updated

4_lifecycle_transitions.invariant: task_status_event and proof_ledger_entry are APPEND-ONLY; a transition is recorded, never overwritten. This preserves full lifecycle history (STORE-001 durable-history class). The blocked_paths clause 'proof history rewrite' is enforced by making proof_ledger_entry insert-only with a monotonic sequence.
4_lifecycle_transitions.mutable_field_authority: Only task_node.status, task_node.proof_uri, task_node.next_action may be updated by a transition. Enforced by a row-level trigger mirroring task_graph.status.json update_policy (allowed_updated_fields = [next_action, proof_uri, status], mutates_source_graph = false).

5_north_star_anchors.description: All five immutable North Star anchors owned by ns_anchor; each is an immutable authored row (NORTH_STAR.md is 'Locked: 2026-07-10'). anchor_coverage owns the anchor->task causal edges.

anchors.required_outcome: LifeOS is a living extension of David's intent.
Company, personal, development, and home form one coherent whole.
David can see and understand the whole.
David can direct it as one system.
LifeOS advances every domain without fragmenting the whole.

5_north_star_anchors.immutability_rule: ns_anchor rows are insert-once, update-forbidden (NORTH_STAR.md immutable-anchor contract). Only coverage_status / decomposition_status / execution_gate are recomputed as derivations from anchor_coverage + task lifecycle; the anchor identity and required_outcome are frozen.
5_north_star_anchors.this_task_covers: POSTGRES-003 appears in the phase_task_ids of NS-WHOLE, NS-CLARITY, and NS-DIRECTION; making the control plane database-native directly serves 'see' (CLARITY), 'direct' (DIRECTION), and 'one coherent whole' (WHOLE).

6_source_provenance.description: Every source-provenance surface has a database owner; SHA-256 content identity is preserved so no lineage is lost (STORE-001 invariant: 'every migrated class keeps its SHA-256 identity and its source commit/PR lineage').

surfaces.owner: source (PK object_id, carries content_sha256, notebook_id, capture_status, proof_uri) + source_task_map for mapped_task_ids
adopted_capability; source_commit_or_pr + current_path + ci_receipt preserve upstream lineage

6_source_provenance.identity_rule: source.content_sha256, adopted_capability.source_commit_or_pr, and proof_ledger_entry.proof_sha256 are the provenance identity keys. A projection is valid only if the recomputed SHA-256 of each projected provenance artifact equals its stored identity.

database_schema.engine: PostgreSQL 16 + RuVector native extension (No-Sidecar), STORE-001 canonical owner

tables.note: 27 phases enumerated verbatim from the sheet
7 rows: draft, ready, running, simulated, complete, blocked, rolled-back
source_row_number is the deterministic CSV ordering key; goal_embedding/gate_embedding are RuVector columns for semantic planning queries
supports multi-parent (LIFEOS-010); ordinal preserves parent order for byte-identical re-join
registry of all target_artifacts + proof + generated files
anchor->task causal edges; ordinal preserves phase_task_ids order
verification_gate must equal task_node.verification_gate (enforced by trigger)
APPEND-ONLY, monotonic sequence; 166 rows; proof_sha256 pins content identity
the deterministic projection registry (see projection_contracts section)
records each envctl projection materialization and whether it was byte-identical

database_schema.no_second_durable_writer: Per STORE-001 invariant 1: PostgreSQL owns every control-plane relation exactly once. redb never becomes a second durable writer for any table above.

projection_contracts.description: Every committed control-plane file is a deterministic, byte-identical, round-trippable projection from PostgreSQL rows via envctl (PostgreSQL -> filesystem, forward-only, disposable). This is the 'deterministic projection contract' the gate requires.
determinism_rules: Stable ORDER BY on a persisted ordinal key (task_node.source_row_number, ns_anchor.anchor_ordinal, proof_ledger_entry.sequence, source.sequence).
Multi-value columns re-serialized with the exact original delimiter '; ' in ordinal order.
CSV projections use RFC4180 QUOTE_ALL, UTF-8, LF line endings, header row verbatim (matches current generated/*.source.csv byte layout).
JSON projections use sorted-or-fixed key order and stable indentation matching the committed file; JSONL preserves one object per line in sequence order.
Every projection run recomputes SHA-256 and compares against projection_contract.expected_sha256; a mismatch fails the projection (information-loss guard).

contracts.source_query: task_node LEFT JOIN task_dependency/task_input/task_target_artifact/task_allowed_path/task_blocked_path (GROUP_CONCAT ... SEPARATOR '; ') ORDER BY source_row_number
contracts.round_trip_test: parse projection -> equals task_node + child tables row-for-row

contracts.source_query: task_node + arrays (parent_ids, inputs, target_artifacts, allowed_paths, blocked_paths) ORDER BY source_row_number
contracts.round_trip_test: normalized round-trips to the same task_node rows

contracts.source_query: derive lifecycle_state per task from task_node.status + proof_ledger_entry + lifecycle_transition; aggregate lifecycle_counts, update_policy, ledger sha
contracts.round_trip_test: derived counts match COUNT(*) GROUP BY derived lifecycle_state

contracts.source_query: ns_anchor LEFT JOIN anchor_coverage (GROUP_CONCAT task_id SEPARATOR '; ') ORDER BY anchor_ordinal
ns_anchor + anchor_coverage + phase; owner_execution_approval from ns_anchor.execution_gate

contracts.serialization: JSONL, one object per line, key order fixed, schema_version lifeos-planning-spine.proof-ledger.v0
contracts.round_trip_test: each line parses to the exact proof_ledger_entry row; recomputed file sha256 = task_graph.status.json.proof_ledger_sha256

contracts.source_query: proof_record + proof_artifact + proof_checksum + proof_live_command WHERE task_id=? and max(revision)
source LEFT JOIN source_task_map ORDER BY sequence
contracts.round_trip_test: parse -> equals source + source_task_map; content_sha256 preserved

no_information_loss_contract.principle: For each control-plane relation, DB -> projection -> parse -> DB must be an identity (round-trip), and projection bytes must equal the committed file (checksum). Together these prove zero information loss.
guarantees: Ordinal preservation: every '; '-delimited value is stored with an ordinal so re-serialization reproduces original order and delimiter exactly.
Verbatim free-text: goal, verification_gate, rollback_plan, owner_agent, required_outcome stored verbatim (no re-wrapping, no normalization).
Boolean fidelity: simulation_required projected as uppercase 'TRUE'/'FALSE' to match the current sheet literal.
Row order fidelity: source_row_number / sequence / anchor_ordinal reproduce the exact committed row order.
Content identity: content_sha256, proof_sha256, source_commit_or_pr preserved; a projection whose recomputed hash != stored identity is rejected.
History fidelity: proof_ledger_entry and task_status_event are append-only; no lifecycle transition is lost or rewritten (blocked_paths 'proof history rewrite' honored).

invariants: PostgreSQL is the single canonical owner of every control-plane relation exactly once (STORE-001 invariant 1).
The committed CSV/JSON/MD surfaces are derived projections, not second authorities; they regenerate deterministically from PostgreSQL via envctl (STORE-001 derived-projections class).
Only status, proof_uri, next_action are mutable per task row (task_graph.status.json update_policy); the authored graph is immutable.
North Star anchor identity and required_outcome are frozen (NORTH_STAR.md immutable-anchor lock 2026-07-10).
Proof ledger and status events are append-only; proof history is never rewritten.
redb holds at most a transient forward buffer during the one-time ingest; it is never a durable control-plane writer.
A projection is valid only if it round-trips AND is byte-identical (checksum) to the committed file.
migration_note: This plan defines the TARGET database ownership and projection contracts. Per the POSTGRES-003 rollback_plan, the currently committed generated/task_graph.source.csv and proof_records/proof_ledger.jsonl remain the canonical editable authority until POSTGRES-010 proves full-user-space parity and executes one explicit verified authority switch. Next action: split one bounded migration task per control-plane table and keep the CSV authoritative until parity passes.
